title: Chapter 1 Mastery Quiz
description: Test your foundational knowledge of Linux philosophy and terminal basics.

questions.question: What is the core component of Linux that communicates directly with the hardware?
questions.explanation: The Kernel is the heart of the OS. The Shell is the interface that allows the user to talk to the Kernel.

questions.question: Linux is case-sensitive, meaning 'File.txt' and 'file.txt' are different files.
questions.explanation: True. Linux filesystems treat uppercase and lowercase letters as distinct characters.

questions.question: You want to read the detailed manual for the 'ls' command. What command do you type?
questions.explanation: 'man ls' opens the manual page for 'ls'. Press 'q' to quit the manual.

questions.question: In the prompt `user@linux:~$`, the symbol `~` represents the user's _____ directory.
questions.explanation: The tilde `~` is a shortcut for the current user's home directory (e.g., /home/user).

questions.question: What does the 'Bash' acronym stand for?
questions.explanation: Bash stands for Bourne Again SHell, a pun on the original Bourne Shell (sh).

questions.question: Linux is closed-source software, meaning you need a license key to use it.
questions.explanation: False. Linux is open-source and freely distributable.

questions.question: You want to print the text 'Hello Cybron' to the terminal. Which command is correct?
questions.explanation: 'echo' is the standard Linux command to display a line of text.

questions.question: In the command structure `command -option argument`, what is the purpose of the `-option`?
options: It modifies the behavior of the command.
It is the program to run.
questions.explanation: Options (or flags) change how the command executes (e.g., `-l` for long listing format in `ls`).

questions.question: To enter a directory named 'My Files', you must use an escape character like this: `cd My\ _____`
questions.explanation: The backslash `\` escapes the space character so the shell treats 'My Files' as one argument instead of two.

questions.question: Pressing the TAB key while typing a command or path will try to auto-complete it.
questions.explanation: True. Tab completion is a massive time-saver in Linux.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a reason hackers prefer Linux?
options: It is open source and auditable.
It allows deep control over the system.
It is the only OS that can run Python.
questions.explanation: Python runs on all major operating systems (Windows, Mac, Linux).

questions.question: What key do you press to quit the `man` page viewer?
questions.explanation: 'q' stands for Quit.

questions.question: What is a 'Shell'?
options: A protective case for the hard drive.
A command interpreter between user and kernel.
questions.explanation: The shell is the software that provides the command-line interface.

questions.question: WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) allows you to run a Linux environment directly within Windows.
questions.explanation: True. Microsoft partnered with Linux distributions to make this possible.

questions.question: In the man page syntax `cp [OPTION]... SOURCE DEST`, which parts are mandatory?
questions.explanation: Items not in square brackets `[]` are required arguments. `SOURCE` and `DEST` are both required.

questions.question: You type `LS` and get 'command not found'. Why?
options: You need to install the 'ls' program.
You are in the wrong directory.
questions.explanation: Typing uppercase `LS` looks for a different program than lowercase `ls`.

questions.question: You must always use quotes with the `echo` command.
questions.explanation: False. `echo Hello` works. Quotes are needed for special characters or multiple spaces.

questions.question: What does the `--help` flag usually do?
options: Displays a quick summary of command options.
questions.explanation: `--help` is a quick reference guide, shorter than the full `man` page.

questions.question: Which character represents the 'root' or superuser prompt?
questions.explanation: The `$` prompt indicates a normal user. The `#` prompt indicates the root superuser (admin).

questions.question: Why is Linux considered 'lightweight'?
options: It physically weighs less than a Windows DVD.
It can run efficiently on old hardware and embedded devices.
It is written in a tiny font.
questions.explanation: Linux is modular and efficient, meaning it doesn't require the latest powerful hardware to run well.
